level_definitions.1: Movement focused - teach the pattern, low irradiation, relaxed body, learn where the joint can and can't go
level_definitions.2: Capsular focused - actively explore end ranges, moderate irradiation, deliberate slowdown at restricted points
level_definitions.3: Irradiation focused - maximum systemic tension, slowest pace, highest neurological demand, drives real articular adaptation

level_selection_logic.new_client: Default to Level 1 regardless of training age
level_selection_logic.cleared_joint_experienced_client: Level 2 or 3 based on block intent (capacity vs adaptation)
level_selection_logic.neurological_gap_flagged: Level 3 - irradiation drives neural drive into missing range
level_selection_logic.pain_on_passive: Stay at Level 1 or regress out of CARs, consider referral

exercises.name: Cervical CAR - Movement Focus
cues: Tall spine, ribs stacked over pelvis
Move slow - you're mapping the joint, not stretching
Only the head moves, shoulders stay still
exercises.notes: Use for assessment and new client pattern learning. Client should feel movement, not effort.

exercises.name: Cervical CAR - Capsular Focus
exercises.position: seated, tall spine, light body tension
cues: Find the restriction and sit in it for a breath
Explore the edges - don't just trace the circle
Chin stays neutral during rotation phases
exercises.typical_dose: 2-3 reps per direction, deliberate at restricted points
exercises.notes: Once pattern is learned, this is where we find capsular restrictions worth working on.

exercises.name: Cervical CAR - Irradiation Focus
cues: Brace everything - legs, trunk, arms - before you move the head
Slowest rep wins
If you're not shaking a little, you're not braced enough
exercises.typical_dose: 1-2 reps per direction, quality over quantity
exercises.notes: Reserved for experienced clients. This is where real adaptation happens at the neck.

exercises.name: Thoracic CAR - Movement Focus
cues: Keep the low back quiet - movement is in the upper back
Reach long through the arm as you rotate
Eyes follow the hand
exercises.notes: Quadruped position lets us isolate T-spine from lumbar compensation.

exercises.name: Thoracic CAR - Capsular Focus
cues: Find the stuck spot in your rotation and breathe into it
Ribcage moves over pelvis, pelvis stays put
No hitching - if you're using shoulder, reset
exercises.typical_dose: 3-5 reps per side with pause at end range
exercises.notes: Most desk-bound clients live here - restricted T-spine rotation drives shoulder and low back compensation.

exercises.name: Thoracic CAR - Irradiation Focus
cues: Grip the floor with your down hand - irradiate from the base
Brace the trunk like you're holding a heavy weight
Own the end range for a two-count before returning
exercises.notes: Pairs well with loaded carries and rotational strength work in the same block.

exercises.name: Lumbar/Global Spine CAR - Movement Focus
cues: Move one segment at a time - not the whole spine together
Breathe with the movement, don't hold
Find the parts that don't want to move
exercises.notes: Global spine CAR - client learns to move spine segmentally, not as one block.

exercises.name: Lumbar/Global Spine CAR - Capsular Focus
cues: Pause at the segment that's stuck and breathe there
Don't cheat with hip movement - keep the pelvis disciplined
Explore the edges of each segment
exercises.typical_dose: 2-3 full sequences with pauses at restricted segments
exercises.notes: Useful for clients with regional stiffness - usually mid-lumbar or lower T-spine.

exercises.name: Lumbar/Global Spine CAR - Irradiation Focus
cues: Brace the whole trunk, then move one vertebra at a time
This should feel hard - that's the point
Slowest rep wins, every time
exercises.notes: Advanced spine control work. Pairs with carries and anti-flexion/anti-extension strength work.

exercises.name: Shoulder CAR - Movement Focus
exercises.position: standing, tall spine, opposite hand on clavicle
cues: Tall spine - shoulder moves, trunk doesn't
Thumb leads the rotation - palm turns with it
Trace the biggest circle you can without cheating
exercises.notes: Baseline shoulder articular screen. Watch for trunk compensation, scapular hiking.

exercises.name: Shoulder CAR - Capsular Focus
exercises.position: standing, tall spine, opposite hand on clavicle
cues: Find the capsule - where does it get tight?
Fist and arm tension moderate - enough to find the edges
Sit in the restriction for a breath before moving through
exercises.typical_dose: 2-3 reps per direction per side with pauses
exercises.notes: Where most mobility-seeking clients live. Identifies anterior/posterior capsule restrictions.

exercises.name: Shoulder CAR - Irradiation Focus
exercises.position: standing, tall spine, full body brace, opposite hand locks clavicle
cues: Fist crushed, arm rigid, whole body locked except the shoulder
Pull down through the lat as you rotate - irradiate from the core
If you can talk through it, you're not trying hard enough
exercises.typical_dose: 1-2 reps per direction per side, maximum intent
exercises.notes: Top-tier shoulder articular work. Pairs with overhead pressing progression and pulling capacity.

exercises.name: Scapular CAR - Movement Focus
cues: Move the shoulder blade, not the arm
Up, back, down, forward - trace the four corners
Keep the arm heavy and passive
exercises.notes: Most adults have no idea how to move their scapulae independently. Start here.

exercises.name: Scapular CAR - Capsular Focus
cues: Reach into each corner - don't short the range
Pause at the hardest position
Keep the neck long - no shrugging compensation
exercises.notes: Protraction and depression are usually the weak points.

exercises.name: Scapular CAR - Irradiation Focus
exercises.position: standing, tall spine, full brace, or in quadruped for more demand
cues: Press the floor away or squeeze the fist hard - irradiate
Own each position for a two-count
Control the return - no flopping
exercises.notes: Pairs with pulling capacity work and overhead pressing prep.

exercises.name: Elbow CAR - Movement Focus
exercises.position: standing, upper arm pinned to side
cues: Upper arm locked to your ribs - only the forearm moves
Supinate and pronate fully at each position
Slow and deliberate
exercises.notes: Often overlooked but important for clients with forearm/wrist complaints.

exercises.name: Elbow CAR - Capsular Focus
cues: Find end range of supination and pronation - explore the capsule
Make a fist to feel the forearm working
Pause at the tightest point
exercises.notes: Relevant for clients with grip issues, pressing discomfort, or history of tennis/golfer's elbow.

exercises.name: Elbow CAR - Irradiation Focus
exercises.position: standing, upper arm pinned, full brace
cues: Crush the fist throughout
Brace the shoulder, pin the upper arm, move only the forearm
Own each end range
exercises.notes: Pairs with grip training and loaded carries.

exercises.name: Wrist CAR - Movement Focus
cues: Forearm still - only the wrist moves
Trace the biggest circle your wrist can make
Fingers relaxed
exercises.notes: Essential for clients doing any loaded pressing or front rack work.

exercises.name: Wrist CAR - Capsular Focus
cues: Close the fist to add tension
Find the stuck points - there will be some
Explore flexion, extension, and deviations at the edges
exercises.notes: Extension is usually the limiting factor - affects front rack, push-ups, pressing.

exercises.name: Wrist CAR - Irradiation Focus
exercises.position: standing or seated, forearm supported, full brace
cues: Fist crushed, forearm rigid, entire arm irradiated
Own each end range for a two-count
Control the return
exercises.notes: Prep for heavy pressing, front squat receiving position, loaded carries.

exercises.name: Hip CAR - Movement Focus
exercises.position: standing, hand on wall or post for balance
cues: Move the femur, not the pelvis
Pelvis stays square - that's the test
Slow - you're mapping, not stretching
exercises.notes: The single most informative CAR you can do. Most clients cheat with pelvis - that's data.

exercises.name: Hip CAR - Capsular Focus
cues: Pelvis locked, femur explores
Find the restricted position and breathe into it
Internal rotation usually hurts first - don't skip it
exercises.typical_dose: 2-3 reps per direction per side with pauses
exercises.notes: Drives most of the table test findings. IR restriction is nearly universal in desk workers.

exercises.name: Hip CAR - Irradiation Focus
exercises.position: standing (hands on wall) or quadruped, full brace
cues: Brace like someone's about to punch you in the gut
Grip the ground with the stance foot - irradiate from below
Move the femur, not the pelvis - this rule never changes
exercises.typical_dose: 1-2 reps per direction per side, max intent
exercises.notes: Pairs with split squat progression, loaded hip hinge, and lower body strength blocks.

exercises.name: Knee CAR - Movement Focus
exercises.position: seated on box or quadruped, hip held still
cues: Only the knee moves - hip stays put
Go through full flexion and extension
Don't force the end range - find it
exercises.notes: Often includes gentle tibial internal/external rotation at end range.

exercises.name: Knee CAR - Capsular Focus
exercises.position: seated on box with hip held, or quadruped
cues: Find full flexion and add tibial rotation at the end
Extend fully and add rotation at the top
Quad engaged throughout
exercises.typical_dose: 3-5 reps per side with rotational work at end ranges
exercises.notes: Tibial rotation capacity is huge for squatting depth and lunging comfort.

exercises.name: Knee CAR - Irradiation Focus
cues: Lock the hip hard, brace the core, drive through the quad
Slowest rep possible
Own full flexion for a breath, full extension with rotation
exercises.notes: Pairs with split squat, step-up, and single-leg strength work.

exercises.name: Ankle CAR - Movement Focus
cues: Circle the whole foot - slow
Big range, not fast range
Keep the knee quiet
exercises.notes: Critical for calf raise capacity and squat/lunge mechanics.

exercises.name: Ankle CAR - Capsular Focus
cues: Find dorsiflexion end range and explore the edges
Invert and evert at each position
The stuck spots are the whole point
exercises.notes: Dorsiflexion restriction limits squat depth and drives knee/hip compensation.

exercises.name: Ankle CAR - Irradiation Focus
cues: Toes gripping, full-body brace
Control every inch of the movement
Own each end range
exercises.notes: Pairs with single-leg calf raise progression and lower body unilateral work.

exercises.name: Toe CAR - Movement Focus
cues: Spread the toes wide
Lift the big toe alone, then lift the other four alone
Circle each toe individually if you can
exercises.notes: Most adults have lost basic toe control. Start here for foot health.

exercises.name: Toe CAR - Capsular Focus
cues: Big toe extension full range - the push-off position
Short-foot the arch as you work the toes
Find the restricted toes and give them extra work
exercises.typical_dose: 5-10 reps per pattern with end range holds
exercises.notes: Big toe extension is critical for gait, calf raise, and any push-off.

exercises.name: Toe CAR - Irradiation Focus
cues: Short-foot hard, whole body braced
Every toe working independently, with tension
This should feel ridiculous and hard - that's right
exercises.notes: Pairs with single-leg calf raise and barefoot balance work.
